mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month increase in industrial production over a three-month period signals expanding manufacturing activity, which positively impacts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns during periods of INDPRO > 0.5% growth over 3 months are not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) compared to periods of decline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.0%, it signals labor market deterioration reducing consumer spending, leading to negative forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, the proportion of positive 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is not significantly below 50% (binomial test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Elevated inflation combined with rising short-term interest rates creates a compressed equity valuation environment. The joint elevation of CPIAUCSL above 3% YoY and FEDFUNDS above 3.5% amplifies the negative impact on forward returns through tighter financial conditions.
failure_condition: The interaction term (CPIAUCSL * FEDFUNDS) in a linear regression predicting 20-day forward returns is not statistically significant (t-stat < 2.0) over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansion characterized by tight labor markets and high capacity utilization, elevated retail sales (RSAFS) signal overheating and imminent recession, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In late-cycle periods (top quartile of industrial capacity utilization), RSAFS > 0.5% monthly growth does not lead to a higher probability of negative 60-day returns compared to late-cycle periods without such retail sales growth (chi-square test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects strong economic fundamentals, which translate into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The directional accuracy of GDP growth sign predicting 5-day forward S&P 500 returns is not significantly above 50% (binomial test p > 0.10) across all quarters in the sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts to below 1,000,000 units (seasonally adjusted annual rate) signals a weakening residential investment sector, often a leading indicator of recession, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST drops below 1,000,000, the median 5-day forward return of S&P 500 is not negative (one-sample sign test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong job growth (PAYEMS monthly change above 0.2%) combined with improving unemployment (UNRATE monthly change below -0.1%) reinforces income and consumption expectations, boosting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction coefficient between PAYEMS and UNRATE in predicting 60-day forward returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.05) in a regression with both main effects and interaction term.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle (FEDFUNDS increased by at least 100 basis points over the past 6 months), further increases in the federal funds rate compress equity valuations due to higher discount rates, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In tightening cycles, the correlation between week-over-week FEDFUNDS changes and 5-day forward S&P 500 returns is not significantly negative (Pearson r > -0.1, p > 0.05).
